title: Fix member login after membership reactivation

requirements.text: Fix the backend member login validation logic to correctly recognize members whose membership status has been changed from paused back to active by an admin

quotes: Member is not able to login after admin marked the membership status as active again after is was paused

acceptanceCriteria: A member whose status was changed from paused to active can successfully log in using their membership ID
The login validation checks the current membership status and does not fail based on historical pause state
Members with active status can authenticate regardless of previous pause history

requirements.text: Ensure the member authentication flow in useMemberAuth.ts properly handles reactivated memberships and does not cache stale pause status

quotes: When member tries to login , it shows login failed. Contact gym if your membership should be active

acceptanceCriteria: The frontend clears any cached authentication state when a member attempts to log in
Login errors for reactivated members are resolved and do not show the 'contact gym' message inappropriately
The authentication hook fetches fresh membership status on each login attempt

requirements.text: Verify that the membership status update flow in the admin MembersTab correctly persists status changes and invalidates relevant queries
acceptanceCriteria: When admin changes a member status from paused to active, the change is immediately reflected in the backend
Query invalidation ensures the updated status is visible across all admin views
No stale cached data prevents the status change from taking effect

constraints: Do not modify the Internet Identity authentication mechanism
Preserve all existing membership status types and enum values
Maintain backward compatibility with existing member records
nonGoals: Changing the UI design of the login pages
Modifying the admin approval workflow for pause requests
